quote: "When looks were fond and words were few."
author: Allan Cunningham

quote: "The sea! the sea! the open sea! The blue, the fresh, the ever free!"
author: Bryan W. Procter

quote: "I never was on the dull, tame shore, But I loved the great sea more and more."
author: Bryan W. Procter

quote: "I dreamt that I dwelt in marble halls, With vassals and serfs at my side."
author: Alfred Bunn

quote: "The light of other days is faded, And all their glories past."
author: Alfred Bunn

quote: "The heart bowed down by weight of woe To weakest hope will cling."
author: Alfred Bunn

quote: "Such graves as his are pilgrim shrines, Shrines to no code or creed confined,-- The Delphian vales, the Palestines, The Meccas of the mind."
author: Fitz-greene Halleck

quote: "There is an evening twilight of the heart, When its wild passion-waves are lulled to rest."
author: Fitz-greene Halleck

quote: "Through life's dark road his sordid way he wends, An incarnation of fat dividends."
author: Charles Sprague

quote: "With hue like that when some great painter dips His pencil in the gloom of earthquake and eclipse."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
source: The Revolt of Islam. Canto v. Stanza 23

quote: "The awful shadow of some unseen Power Floats, tho' unseen, amongst us."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "A pard-like spirit, beautiful and swift."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "Kings are like stars,--they rise and set, they have The worship of the world, but no repose."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "What! alive, and so bold, O earth?"
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
source: Written on hearing the News of the Death of Napoleon

quote: "Those who inflict must suffer, for they see The work of their own hearts, and this must be Our chastisement or recompense."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "Most wretched men Are cradled into poetry by wrong: They learn in suffering what they teach in song."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "I could lie down like a tired child, And weep away the life of care Which I have borne, and yet must bear."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
source: Stanzas written in Dejection, near Naples. Stanza 4

quote: "A lovely lady, garmented in light From her own beauty."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "I love tranquil solitude And such society As is quiet, wise, and good."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "The desire of the moth for the star, Of the night for the morrow, The devotion to something afar From the sphere of our sorrow."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
source: One Word is too often profaned

quote: "You lie--under a mistake, For this is the most civil sort of lie That can be given to a man's face. I now Say what I think."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
source: Translation of Calderon's Magico Prodigioso. Scene i

quote: "Poets are the hierophants of an unapprehended inspiration; the mirrors of the gigantic shadows which futurity casts upon the present."
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

quote: "The trivial round, the common task, Would furnish all we ought to ask."
author: John Keble

quote: "The breaking waves dashed high On a stern and rock-bound coast, And the woods against a stormy sky Their giant branches tossed."
author: Felicia D. Hemans

quote: "Through the laburnum's dropping gold Rose the light shaft of Orient mould, And Europe's violets, faintly sweet, Purpled the mossbeds at its feet."
author: Felicia D. Hemans

quote: "They grew in beauty side by side, They filled one home with glee: Their graves are severed far and wide By mount and stream and sea."
author: Felicia D. Hemans

quote: "Come to the sunset tree! The day is past and gone; The woodman's axe lies free, And the reaper's work is done."
author: Felicia D. Hemans

quote: "Oh, call my brother back to me! I cannot play alone: The summer comes with flower and bee,-- Where is my brother gone?"
author: Felicia D. Hemans

quote: "I have looked on the hills of the stormy North, And the larch has hung his tassels forth."
author: Felicia D. Hemans

quote: "I had a hat. It was not all a hat,-- Part of the brim was gone: Yet still I wore it on."
author: Felicia D. Hemans
source: Rhine Song of the German Soldiers after Victory

quote: "To him who in the love of Nature holds Communion with her visible forms, she speaks A various language."
author: William Cullen Bryant

quote: "Go forth under the open sky, and list To Nature's teachings."
author: William Cullen Bryant

quote: "The hills, Rock-ribbed, and ancient as the sun."
author: William Cullen Bryant

quote: "Old ocean's gray and melancholy waste."
author: William Cullen Bryant

quote: "All that tread The globe are but a handful to the tribes That slumber in its bosom."
author: William Cullen Bryant

quote: "The stormy March has come at last, With winds and clouds and changing skies; I hear the rushing of the blast That through the snowy valley flies."
author: William Cullen Bryant

quote: "Loveliest of lovely things are they On earth that soonest pass away. The rose that lives its little hour Is prized beyond the sculptured flower."
author: William Cullen Bryant
source: A Scene on the Banks of the Hudson

quote: "A thing of beauty is a joy forever; Its loveliness increases; it will never Pass into nothingness."
author: John Keats

quote: "Love in a hut, with water and a crust, Is--Love, forgive us!--cinders, ashes, dust."
author: John Keats

quote: "The silver snarling trumpets 'gan to chide."
author: John Keats
source: The Eve of St. Agnes. Stanza 4

quote: "Asleep in lap of legends old."
author: John Keats
source: The Eve of St. Agnes. Stanza 15

quote: "Sudden a thought came like a full-blown rose, Flushing his brow."
author: John Keats
source: The Eve of St. Agnes. Stanza 16

quote: "A poor, weak, palsy-stricken, churchyard thing."
author: John Keats
source: The Eve of St. Agnes. Stanza 18

quote: "As though a rose should shut and be a bud again."
author: John Keats
source: The Eve of St. Agnes. Stanza 27

quote: "Those green-robed senators of mighty woods, Tall oaks, branch-charmed by the earnest stars, Dream, and so dream all night without a stir."
author: John Keats

quote: "The days of peace and slumberous calm are fled."
author: John Keats

quote: "Literary men are . . . a perpetual priesthood."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: State of German Literature. Edinburgh Review, 1827

quote: "Clever men are good, but they are not the best."
author: Thomas Carlyle

quote: "How does the poet speak to men with power, but by being still more a man than they?"
author: Thomas Carlyle

quote: "A poet without love were a physical and metaphysical impossibility."
author: Thomas Carlyle

quote: "His religion at best is an anxious wish,--like that of Rabelais, a great Perhaps."
author: Thomas Carlyle

quote: "To the very last, he [Napoleon] had a kind of idea; that, namely, of _la carrière ouverte aux talents_,--the tools to him that can handle them."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Sir Walter Scott. London and Westminster Review, 1838

quote: "The eye of the intellect "sees in all objects what it brought with it the means of seeing."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Varnhagen Von Ense's Memoirs. London and Westminster Review, 1838

quote: "Happy the people whose annals are blank in history-books."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Life of Frederick the Great. Book xvi. Chap. i

quote: "The greatest of faults, I should say, is to be conscious of none."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Heroes and Hero-Worship. The Hero as a Prophet

quote: "The true University of these days is a Collection of Books."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Heroes and Hero-Worship. The Hero as a Man of Letters

quote: "One life,--a little gleam of time between two Eternities."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Heroes and Hero-Worship. The Hero as a Man of Letters

quote: "Adversity is sometimes hard upon a man; but for one man who can stand prosperity there are a hundred that will stand adversity."
author: Thomas Carlyle
source: Heroes and Hero-Worship. The Hero as a Man of Letters

quote: "I want you to see Peel, Stanley, Graham, Sheil, Russell, Macaulay, Old Joe, and so on. They are all upper-crust here."
author: Thomas C. Haliburton

quote: "I 've wandered east, I 've wandered west, Through many a weary way; But never, never can forget The love of life's young day."
author: William Motherwell

quote: "And we, with Nature's heart in tune, Concerted harmonies."
author: William Motherwell

quote: "I 'd be a butterfly born in a bower, Where roses and lilies and violets meet."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "Oh no! we never mention her,-- Her name is never heard; My lips are now forbid to speak That once familiar word."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly
source: Oh no! we never mention her

quote: "Gayly the troubadour Touched his guitar."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "Why don't the men propose, Mamma? Why don't the men propose?"
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "She wore a wreath of roses The night that first we met."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "Friends depart, and memory takes them To her caverns, pure and deep."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "Tell me the tales that to me were so dear, Long, long ago, long, long ago."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "The rose that all are praising Is not the rose for me."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly
source: The Rose that all are praising

quote: "The mistletoe hung in the castle hall, The holly-branch shone on the old oak wall."
author: Thomas Haynes Bayly

quote: "A baby was sleeping, Its mother was weeping."
author: Samuel Lover

quote: "Reproof on her lips, but a smile in her eye."
author: Samuel Lover

quote: "Then here goes another," says he, "to make sure, For there 's luck in odd numbers," says Rory O'More."
author: Samuel Lover

quote: "There was a place in childhood that I remember well, And there a voice of sweetest tone bright fairy tales did tell."
author: Samuel Lover

quote: "Sure the shovel and tongs To each other belongs."
author: Samuel Lover

quote: "There 's not a string attuned to mirth But has its chord in melancholy."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "Straight down the crooked lane, And all round the square."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "A man that 's fond precociously of _stirring_ Must be a spoon."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "Gold! Gold! Gold! Gold! Bright and yellow, hard and cold."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "Another tumble! That 's his precious nose!"
author: Thomas Hood
source: Parental Ode to my Infant Son

quote: "With fingers weary and worn, With eyelids heavy and red, A woman sat in unwomanly rags Plying her needle and thread,-- Stitch! Stitch! Stitch!"
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "O men with sisters dear, O men with mothers and wives, It is not linen you 're wearing out, But human creatures' lives!"
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "Sewing at once a double thread, A shroud as well as a shirt."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "My tears must stop, for every drop Hinders needle and thread."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "Take her up tenderly, Lift her with care; Fashioned so slenderly, Young, and so fair!"
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "The Quaker loves an ample brim, A hat that bows to no salaam; And dear the beaver is to him As if it never made a dam."
author: Thomas Hood

quote: "He laid his hand upon "the Ocean's mane," And played familiar with his hoary locks."
author: Robert Pollok
source: The Course of Time. Book iv. Line 389

quote: "With one hand he put A penny in the urn of poverty, And with the other took a shilling out."
author: Robert Pollok
source: The Course of Time. Book viii. Line 632

quote: "We join ourselves to no party that does not carry the flag and keep step to the music of the Union."
author: Rufus Choate

quote: "Its constitution the glittering and sounding generalities of natural right which make up the Declaration of Independence."
author: Rufus Choate
source: Letter to the Maine Whig Committee, 1856

quote: "He stood beside a cottage lone And listened to a lute, One summer's eve, when the breeze was gone, And the nightingale was mute."
author: Thomas K. Hervey

quote: "Like ships, that sailed for sunny isles, But never came to shore."
author: Thomas K. Hervey

quote: "That is the best government which desires to make the people happy, and knows how to make them happy."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "Free trade, one of the greatest blessings which a government can confer on a people, is in almost every country unpopular."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "The history of nations, in the sense in which I use the word, is often best studied in works not professedly historical."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "We hold that the most wonderful and splendid proof of genius is a great poem produced in a civilized age."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "Nobles by the right of an earlier creation, and priests by the imposition of a mightier hand."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "His imagination resembled the wings of an ostrich. It enabled him to run, though not to soar."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "He had a head which statuaries loved to copy, and a foot the deformity of which the beggars in the streets mimicked."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay
source: On Moore's Life of Lord Byron. 1830

quote: "We know no spectacle so ridiculous as the British public in one of its periodical fits of morality."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay
source: On Moore's Life of Lord Byron. 1830

quote: "That wonderful book, while it obtains admiration from the most fastidious critics, is loved by those who are too simple to admire it."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "The conformation of his mind was such that whatever was little seemed to him great, and whatever was great seemed to him little."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "Temple was a man of the world amongst men of letters, a man of letters amongst men of the world."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "The chief-justice was rich, quiet, and infamous."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "I shall cheerfully bear the reproach of having descended below the dignity of history."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay
source: History of England. Vol. i. Chap. i

quote: "There were gentlemen and there were seamen in the navy of Charles II. But the seamen were not gentlemen, and the gentlemen were not seamen."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay
source: History of England. Vol. i. Chap. ii

quote: "The Puritan hated bear-baiting, not because it gave pain to the bear, but because it gave pleasure to the spectators."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay
source: History of England. Vol. i. Chap. iii

quote: "I have not the Chancellor's encyclopedic mind. He is indeed a kind of semi-Solomon. He _half_ knows everything, from the cedar to the hyssop."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "How well Horatius kept the bridge In the brave days of old."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay
source: Lays of Ancient Rome. Horatius, lxx

quote: "The sweeter sound of woman's praise."
author: Thomas B. Macaulay

quote: "The world knows nothing of its greatest men."
author: J. A. Wade
source: Philip Van Artevelde. Part i. Act i. Sc. 5

quote: "An unreflected light did never yet Dazzle the vision feminine."
author: J. A. Wade
source: Philip Van Artevelde. Part i. Act i. Sc. 5

quote: "There is a higher law than the Constitution."
author: William H. Seward

quote: "It is an irrepressible conflict between opposing and enduring forces."
author: William H. Seward

quote: "Twelve years ago I was a boy, A happy boy at Drury's."
author: W. M. Praed

quote: "Some lie beneath the churchyard stone, And some before the speaker."
author: W. M. Praed

quote: "I remember, I remember How my childhood fleeted by,-- The mirth of its December And the warmth of its July."
author: W. M. Praed

quote: "Near the lake where drooped the willow, Long time ago!"
author: George P. Morris

quote: "He is one of those wise philanthropists who in a time of famine would vote for nothing but a supply of toothpicks."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "The surest way to hit a woman's heart is to take aim kneeling."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "That fellow would vulgarize the day of judgment."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "The best thing I know between France and England is the sea."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "Some people are so fond of ill-luck that they run half-way to meet it."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "There is something about a wedding-gown prettier than in any other gown in the world."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "He was so good he would pour rose-water on a toad."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "As for the brandy, "nothing extenuate;" and the water, put nought in in malice."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "Talk to him of Jacob's ladder, and he would ask the number of the steps."
author: Douglas Jerrold

quote: "Not from a vain or shallow thought His awful Jove young Phidias brought."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Out from the heart of Nature rolled The burdens of the Bible old."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Earth proudly wears the Parthenon As the best gem upon her zone."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "If eyes were made for seeing, Then Beauty is its own excuse for being."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Things are in the saddle, And ride mankind."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Ode, inscribed to W. H. Channing

quote: "Love not the flower they pluck and know it not, And all their botany is Latin names."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The silent organ loudest chants The master's requiem."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "And every man, in love or pride, Of his fate is never wide."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "None shall rule but the humble, And none but Toil shall have."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Oh, tenderly the haughty day Fills his blue urn with fire."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Go put your creed into your deed, Nor speak with double tongue."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Born for success he seemed, With grace to win, with heart to hold, With shining gifts that took all eyes."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "For what avail the plough or sail, Or land or life, if freedom fail?"
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Time dissipates to shining ether the solid angularity of facts."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Nature is a mutable cloud which is always and never the same."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "A man is a bundle of relations, a knot of roots, whose flower and fruitage is the world."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The virtue in most request is conformity. Self-reliance is its aversion. It loves not realities and creators, but names and customs."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "To be great is to be misunderstood."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Discontent is the want of self-reliance: it is infirmity of will."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Everything in Nature contains all the powers of Nature. Everything is made of one hidden stuff."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Every action is measured by the depth of the sentiment from which it proceeds."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "A friend may well be reckoned the masterpiece of Nature."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "There is nothing settled in manners, but the laws of behaviour yield to the energy of the individual."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "He is great who is what he is from Nature, and who never reminds us of others."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Representative Men. Uses of Great Men

quote: "Thought is the property of him who can entertain it, and of him who can adequately place it."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The hearing ear is always found close to the speaking tongue."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "I find the Englishman to be him of all men who stands firmest in his shoes."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "A creative economy is the fuel of magnificence."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The manly part is to do with might and main what you can do."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The alleged power to charm down insanity, or ferocity in beasts, is a power behind the eye."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Fine manners need the support of fine manners in others."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "Good is a good doctor, but Bad is sometimes a better."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: The Conduct of Life. Considerations by the Way

quote: "We do not count a man's years until he has nothing else to count."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: The Conduct of Life. Old Age

quote: "Life is not so short but that there is always time enough for courtesy."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Letters and Social Aims. Social Aims

quote: "By necessity, by proclivity, and by delight, we all quote."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Letters and Social Aims. Quotation and Originality

quote: "Next to the originator of a good sentence is the first quoter of it."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Letters and Social Aims. Quotation and Originality

quote: "In fact, it is as difficult to appropriate the thoughts of others as it is to invent."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Letters and Social Aims. Quotation and Originality

quote: "The passages of Shakespeare that we most prize were never quoted until within this century."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Letters and Social Aims. Quotation and Originality

quote: "Great men are they who see that spiritual is stronger than any material force; that thoughts rule the world."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
source: Progress of Culture. Phi Beta Kappa Address, July 18, 1867

quote: "My country is the world; my countrymen are mankind."
author: William Lloyd Garrison

quote: "Our country is the world; our countrymen are mankind."
author: William Lloyd Garrison
source: Motto of the Liberator, Vol. i. No. 1, 1831

quote: "I will be as harsh as truth and as uncompromising as justice."
author: William Lloyd Garrison

quote: "Our country is the world; our countrymen are all mankind."
author: William Lloyd Garrison

quote: "Old England is our home, and Englishmen are we; Our tongue is known in every clime, our flag in every sea."
author: Mary Howitt

quote: "In the lexicon of youth, which fate reserves For a bright manhood, there is no such word As "fail."
author: Edward Bulwer Lytton

quote: "The brilliant chief, irregularly great, Frank, haughty, rash,--the Rupert of debate!"
author: Edward Bulwer Lytton

quote: "The man who smokes, thinks like a sage and acts like a Samaritan."
author: Edward Bulwer Lytton

quote: "Free trade is not a principle, it is an expedient."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "A conservative government is an organized hypocrisy."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "It is much easier to be critical than to be correct."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The characteristic of the present age is craving credulity."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Assassination has never changed the history of the world."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The secret of success is constancy to purpose."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The author who speaks about his own books is almost as bad as a mother who talks about her own children."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Apologies only account for that which they do not alter."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Increased means and increased leisure are the two civilizers of man."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Man is not the creature of circumstances. Circumstances are the creatures of men."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)
source: Vivian Grey. Book vi. Chap. vii

quote: "Patience is a necessary ingredient of genius."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)
source: Contarini Fleming. Part iv. Chap. v

quote: "Youth is a blunder; manhood a struggle; old age a regret."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "But what minutes! Count them by sensation, and not by calendars, and each moment is a day, and the race a life."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Only think of Cockie Graves having gone and done it!"
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The Duke of Wellington brought to the post of first minister immortal fame,--a quality of success which would almost seem to include all others."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The Egremonts had never said anything that was remembered, or done anything that could be recalled."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "That earliest shock in one's life which occurs to all of us; which first makes us think."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "To be conscious that you are ignorant is a great step to knowledge."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Principle is ever my motto, not expediency."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Property has its duties as well as its rights."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "Mr. Kremlin was distinguished for ignorance; for he had only one idea, and that was wrong."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "That when a man fell into his anecdotage, it was a sign for him to retire."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "You know who critics are?--the men who have failed in literature and art."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The Athanasian Creed is the most splendid ecclesiastical lyric ever poured forth by the genius of man."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The world is a wheel, and it will all come round right."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "The sweet simplicity of the three per cents."
author: Benjamin Disraeli (earl Beaconsfield)

quote: "A word in season spoken May calm the troubled breast."
author: Charles Jefferys

quote: "The bud is on the bough again, The leaf is on the tree."
author: Charles Jefferys
source: The Meeting of Spring and Summer

quote: "We have lived and loved together Through many changing years; We have shared each other's gladness, And wept each other's tears."
author: Charles Jefferys
source: We have lived and loved together

quote: "I 'm sitting on the stile, Mary, Where we sat side by side."
author: Lady Dufferin

quote: "I 'm very lonely now, Mary, For the poor make no new friends; But oh they love the better still The few our Father sends!"
author: Lady Dufferin

quote: "Lives of great men all remind us We can make our lives sublime, And departing, leave behind us Footprints on the sands of time."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "Let us, then, be up and doing, With a heart for any fate; Still achieving, still pursuing, Learn to labour and to wait."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "The star of the unconquered will."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "The hooded clouds, like friars, Tell their beads in drops of rain."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "No tears Dim the sweet look that Nature wears."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "No one is so accursed by fate, No one so utterly desolate, But some heart, though unknown, Responds unto his own."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "Into each life some rain must fall, Some days must be dark and dreary."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "Standing with reluctant feet Where the brook and river meet, Womanhood and childhood fleet!"
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "She floats upon the river of his thoughts."
author: Henry W. Longfellow
source: The Spanish Student. Act ii. Sc. 3

quote: "A banner with the strange device."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "This is the place. Stand still, my steed,-- Let me review the scene, And summon from the shadowy past The forms that once have been."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "The day is done, and the darkness Falls from the wings of Night, As a feather is wafted downward From an eagle in his flight."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "A feeling of sadness and longing That is not akin to pain, And resembles sorrow only As the mist resembles the rain."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "And the night shall be filled with music, And the cares that infest the day Shall fold their tents like the Arabs, And as silently steal away."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "When she had passed, it seemed like the ceasing of exquisite music."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "Into a world unknown,--the corner-stone of a nation!"
author: Henry W. Longfellow
source: The Courtship of Miles Standish. iv

quote: "The heights by great men reached and kept Were not attained by sudden flight, But they while their companions slept Were toiling upward in the night."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "The surest pledge of a deathless name Is the silent homage of thoughts unspoken."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "The love of learning, the sequestered nooks, And all the sweet serenity of books."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "With useless endeavour Forever, forever, Is Sisyphus rolling His stone up the mountain!"
author: Henry W. Longfellow
source: The Masque of Pandora. Chorus of the Eumenides

quote: "All things come round to him who will but wait."
author: Henry W. Longfellow
source: Tales of a Wayside Inn. The Student's Tale

quote: "Time has laid his hand Upon my heart gently, not smiting it, But as a harper lays his open palm Upon his harp, to deaden its vibrations."
author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: "The Constitution, in all its provisions, looks to an indestructible Union composed of indestructible States."
author: Salmon P. Chase
source: Decision in Texas v. White, 7 Wallace, 725

quote: "No more slave States; no slave Territories."
author: Salmon P. Chase
source: Platform of the Free Soil National Convention, 1848

quote: "The way to resumption is to resume."
author: Salmon P. Chase

quote: "There Shakespeare, on whose forehead climb The crowns o' the world; oh, eyes sublime With tears and laughter for all time!"
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

quote: "Oh, the little birds sang east, and the little birds sang west."
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

quote: "She has seen the mystery hid Under Egypt's pyramid: By those eyelids pale and close Now she knows what Rhamses knows."
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

quote: "I believe this government cannot endure permanently half slave and half free."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "Let us have faith that right makes might; and in that faith let us dare to do our duty as we understand it."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "In giving freedom to the slave we assure freedom to the free,--honorable alike in what we give and what we preserve."
author: Abraham Lincoln
source: Second Annual Message to Congress, Dec. 1, 1862

quote: "I have called this principle, by which each slight variation, if useful, is preserved, by the term Natural Selection."
author: Charles Darwin
source: The Origin of Species. Chap. iii

quote: "We will now discuss in a little more detail the Struggle for Existence."
author: Charles Darwin
source: The Origin of Species. Chap. iii

quote: "The expression often used by Mr. Herbert Spencer of the Survival of the Fittest is more accurate, and is sometimes equally convenient."
author: Charles Darwin
source: The Origin of Species. Chap. iii

quote: "Great thoughts, great feelings came to them, Like instincts, unawares."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "A man's best things are nearest him, Lie close about his feet."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "I wandered by the brookside, I wandered by the mill; I could not hear the brook flow, The noisy wheel was still."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "The beating of my own heart Was all the sound I heard."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "Ay, tear her tattered ensign down! Long has it waved on high, And many an eye has danced to see That banner in the sky."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "Like sentinel and nun, they keep Their vigil on the green."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "Their discords sting through Burns and Moore, Like hedgehogs dressed in lace."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "When the last reader reads no more."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "The freeman casting with unpurchased hand The vote that shakes the turrets of the land."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "And when you stick on conversation's burrs, Don't strew your pathway with those dreadful _urs_."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "O hearts that break and give no sign Save whitening lip and fading tresses!"
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)

quote: "A thought is often original, though you have uttered it a hundred times."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. i

quote: "People that make puns are like wanton boys that put coppers on the railroad tracks."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. i

quote: "Everybody likes and respects self-made men. It is a great deal better to be made in that way than not to be made at all."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. i

quote: "The axis of the earth sticks out visibly through the centre of each and every town or city."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. vi

quote: "The world's great men have not commonly been great scholars, nor its great scholars great men."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. vi

quote: "Knowledge and timber should n't be much used till they are seasoned."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. vi

quote: "The hat is the _ultimum moriens_ of respectability."
author: Richard Monckton Milnes (lord Houghton)
source: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table. viii

quote: "Her suffering ended with the day, Yet lived she at its close, And breathed the long, long night away In statue-like repose."
author: James Aldrich

quote: "But when the sun in all his state Illumed the eastern skies, She passed through Glory's morning-gate, And walked in Paradise."
author: James Aldrich

quote: "It came upon the midnight clear, That glorious song of old."
author: Edmund H. Sears

quote: "A babe in a house is a well-spring of pleasure."
author: Martin F. Tupper

quote: "Perched upon a bust of Pallas, just above my chamber door,-- Perched, and sat, and nothing more."
author: Edgar A. Poe

quote: "Whom unmerciful disaster Followed fast and followed faster."
author: Edgar A. Poe

quote: "To the glory that was Greece And the grandeur that was Rome."
author: Edgar A. Poe

quote: "Revolutions are not made; they come."
author: Wendell Phillips

quote: "What the Puritans gave the world was not thought, but action."
author: Wendell Phillips

quote: "Every man meets his Waterloo at last."
author: Wendell Phillips

quote: "Any nose May ravage with impunity a rose."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "The sad rhyme of the men who proudly clung To their first fault, and withered in their pride."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Progress is The law of life: man is not Man as yet."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Some unsuspected isle in the far seas,-- Some unsuspected isle in far-off seas."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "There 's a woman like a dewdrop, she 's so purer than the purest."
author: Robert Browning
source: A Blot in the 'Scutcheon. Act i. Sc. iii

quote: "When is man strong until he feels alone?"
author: Robert Browning

quote: "When the fight begins within himself, A man 's worth something."
author: Robert Browning
source: Men and Women. Bishop Blougram's Apology

quote: "The sprinkled isles, Lily on lily, that o'erlace the sea."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Rafael made a century of sonnets."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Over my head his arm he flung Against the world."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Oh never star Was lost here but it rose afar."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "When the liquor 's out, why clink the cannikin?"
author: Robert Browning
source: The Flight of the Duchess. xvi

quote: "Lofty designs must close in like effects."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Lost, lost! one moment knelled the woe of years."
author: Robert Browning
source: Childe Roland to the Dark Tower came. xxxiii

quote: "Just for a handful of silver he left us, Just for a riband to stick in his coat."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "They are perfect; how else?--they shall never change: We are faulty; why not?--we have time in store."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Italy, my Italy! Queen Mary's saying serves for me (When fortune's malice Lost her Calais): "Open my heart, and you will see Graved inside of it 'Italy.'"
author: Robert Browning

quote: "O woman-country! wooed not wed, Loved all the more by earth's male-lands, Laid to their hearts instead."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "That great brow And the spirit-small hand propping it."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "If two lives join, there is oft a scar. They are one and one, with a shadowy third; One near one is too far."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Only I discern Infinite passion, and the pain Of finite hearts that yearn."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "Ah, did you once see Shelley plain, And did he stop and speak to you, And did you speak to him again? How strange it seems, and new!"
author: Robert Browning

quote: "What I aspired to be, And was not, comforts me."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "How sad and bad and mad it was! But then, how it was sweet!"
author: Robert Browning

quote: "So may a glory from defect arise."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "This could but have happened once,-- And we missed it, lost it forever."
author: Robert Browning

quote: "In the great right of an excessive wrong."
author: Robert Browning
source: The Ring and the Book. The other Half-Rome. Line 1055

quote: "Was never evening yet But seemed far beautifuller than its day."
author: Robert Browning
source: The Ring and the Book. Pompilia. Line 357

quote: "The curious crime, the fine Felicity and flower of wickedness."
author: Robert Browning
source: The Ring and the Book. The Pope. Line 590

quote: "Why comes temptation, but for man to meet And master and make crouch beneath his foot, And so be pedestaled in triumph?"
author: Robert Browning
source: The Ring and the Book. The Pope. Line 1185

quote: "White shall not neutralize the black, nor good Compensate bad in man, absolve him so: Life's business being just the terrible choice."
author: Robert Browning
source: The Ring and the Book. The Pope. Line 1236

quote: "It is the glory and good of Art That Art remains the one way possible Of speaking truth,--to mouths like mine, at least."
author: Robert Browning
source: The Book and the Ring. The Pope. Line 842

quote: "But how carve way i' the life that lies before, If bent on groaning ever for the past?"
author: Robert Browning

quote: "With this same key Shakespeare unlocked his heart" once more! Did Shakespeare? If so, the less Shakespeare he!"
author: Robert Browning

quote: "A demd, damp, moist, unpleasant body!"
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "My life is one demd horrid grind."
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "He 's tough, ma'am,--tough is J. B.; tough and devilish sly."
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "The bearings of this observation lays in the application on it."
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "Papa, potatoes, poultry, prunes and prism, all very good words for the lips,--especially prunes and prism."
author: Charles Dickens
source: Little Dorrit. Book ii. Chap. v

quote: "Whatever was required to be done, the Circumlocution Office was beforehand with all the public departments in the art of perceiving HOW NOT TO DO IT."
author: Charles Dickens
source: Little Dorrit. Book ii. Chap. x

quote: "In came Mrs. Fezziwig, one vast substantial smile."
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "We are spirits clad in veils; Man by man was never seen; All our deep communing fails To remove the shadowy screen."
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "Labour itself is but a sorrowful song, The protest of the weak against the strong."
author: F. W. Faber

quote: "Poets are all who love, who feel great truths, And tell them; and the truth of truths is love."
author: Ellen Sturgis Hooper
source: Scene, Another and a Better World

quote: "America! half-brother of the world! With something good and bad of every land."
author: Ellen Sturgis Hooper

quote: "I love it, I love it, and who shall dare To chide me for loving that old arm-chair?"
author: Ellen Sturgis Hooper

quote: "How cruelly sweet are the echoes that start When memory plays an old tune on the heart!"
author: Ellen Sturgis Hooper

quote: "At present there is no distinction among the upper ten thousand of the city."
author: Nathaniel P. Willis

quote: "It is the month of June, The month of leaves and roses, When pleasant sights salute the eyes, And pleasant scents the noses."
author: Nathaniel P. Willis

quote: "To win the secret of a weed's plain heart."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Endurance is the crowning quality, And patience all the passion of great hearts."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "One day with life and heart Is more than time enough to find a world."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Truth forever on the scaffold, Wrong forever on the throne."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Dear common flower, that grow'st beside the way, Fringing the dusty road with harmless gold."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "The thing we long for, that we are For one transcendent moment."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "She doeth little kindnesses Which most leave undone, or despise."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Who gives himself with his alms feeds three,-- Himself, his hungering neighbor, and me."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Vision of Sir Launfal. Part Second. viii

quote: "There comes Emerson first, whose rich words, every one, Are like gold nails in temples to hang trophies on."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Nature fits all her children with something to do."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Laborin' man an' laborin' woman Hev one glory an' one shame; Ev'y thin' thet 's done inhuman Injers all on 'em the same."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. First Series. No. i

quote: "I _don't_ believe in princerple, But oh I _du_ in interest."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. First Series. No. vi

quote: "Of my merit On thet pint you yourself may jedge; All is, I never drink no sperit, Nor I haint never signed no pledge."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. First Series. No. vii

quote: "Ez to my princerples, I glory In hevin' nothin' o' the sort."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. First Series. No. vii

quote: "Zekle crep' up quite unbeknown An' peeked in thru' the winder, An' there sot Huldy all alone, 'Ith no one nigh to hender."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. The Courtin'

quote: "The very room, coz she was in, Seemed warm from floor to ceilin'."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. The Courtin'

quote: "His heart kep' goin' pity-pat, But hern went pity-Zekle."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. The Courtin'

quote: "All kin' o' smily round the lips, An' teary round the lashes."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. The Courtin'

quote: "Like streams that keep a summer mind Snow-hid in Jenooary."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. The Courtin'

quote: "Our Pilgrim stock wuz pithed with hardihood."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. No. vi

quote: "Soft-heartedness, in times like these, Shows sof'ness in the upper story."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. No. vii

quote: "Under the yaller pines I house, When sunshine makes 'em all sweet-scented, An' hear among their furry boughs The baskin' west-wind purr contented."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: The Biglow Papers. Second Series. No. x

quote: "From lower to the higher next, Not to the top, is Nature's text; And embryo Good, to reach full stature, Absorbs the Evil in its nature."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Here was a type of the true elder race, And one of Plutarch's men talked with us face to face."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Ode at the Harvard Commemoration, July 21, 1865

quote: "Safe in the hallowed quiets of the past."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "The one thing finished in this hasty world."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Solitude is as needful to the imagination as society is wholesome for the character."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. Dryden

quote: "A wise scepticism is the first attribute of a good critic."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. Shakespeare Once More

quote: "One thorn of experience is worth a whole wilderness of warning."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. Shakespeare Once More

quote: "Aspiration sees only one side of every question; possession many."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. New England Two Centuries ago

quote: "Truly there is a tide in the affairs of men; but there is no gulf-stream setting forever in one direction."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. New England Two Centuries ago

quote: "There is no better ballast for keeping the mind steady on its keel, and saving it from all risk of crankiness, than business."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. New England Two Centuries ago

quote: "Puritanism, believing itself quick with the seed of religious liberty, laid, without knowing it, the egg of democracy."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. New England Two Centuries ago

quote: "Talent is that which is in a man's power; genius is that in whose power a man is."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. Rousseau and the Sentimentalists

quote: "Sentiment is intellectualized emotion,--emotion precipitated, as it were, in pretty crystals by the fancy."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. First Series. Rousseau and the Sentimentalists

quote: "In all literary history there is no such figure as Dante, no such homogeneousness of life and works, such loyalty to ideas, such sublime irrecognition of the unessential."
author: James Russell Lowell
source: Among my Books. Second Series. Dante

quote: "The only faith that wears well and holds its color in all weathers, is that which is woven of conviction and set with the sharp mordant of experience."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "It is by presence of mind in untried emergencies that the native metal of a man is tested."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "What a sense of security in an old book which Time has criticised for us!"
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "There is no good in arguing with the inevitable. The only argument available with an east wind is to put on your overcoat."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "Let us be of good cheer, however, remembering that the misfortunes hardest to bear are those which never come."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "A great man is made up of qualities that meet or make great occasions."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "It ["The Ancient Mariner"] is marvellous in its mastery over that delightfully fortuitous inconsequence that is the adamantine logic of dreamland."
author: James Russell Lowell

quote: "O Mary, go and call the cattle home, And call the cattle home, And call the cattle home, Across the sands o' Dee!"
author: Charles Kingsley

quote: "Men must work, and women must weep."
author: Charles Kingsley

quote: "The world goes up and the world goes down, And the sunshine follows the rain; And yesterday's sneer and yesterday's frown Can never come over again."
author: Charles Kingsley

quote: "No other terms than unconditional and immediate surrender. I propose to move immediately upon your works."
author: Ulysses S. Grant
source: To Gen. S. B. Buckner, Fort Donelson, Feb. 16, 1862

quote: "I propose to fight it out on this line, if it takes all summer."
author: Ulysses S. Grant
source: Despatch to Washington. Before Spottsylvania Court House, May 11, 1864

quote: "I know no method to secure the repeal of bad or obnoxious laws so effectual as their strict construction."
author: Ulysses S. Grant

author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Silence is the best resolve for him who distrusts himself."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "A man who is ungrateful is often less to blame than his benefactor."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The understanding is always the dupe of the heart."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Nothing is given so profusely as advice."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The true way to be deceived is to think oneself more knowing than others."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Usually we praise only to be praised."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Our repentance is not so much regret for the ill we have done as fear of the ill that may happen to us in consequence."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Most people judge men only by success or by fortune."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Hypocrisy is the homage vice pays to virtue."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Too great haste to repay an obligation is a kind of ingratitude."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "There is great ability in knowing how to conceal one's ability."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The pleasure of love is in loving. We are happier in the passion we feel than in that we inspire."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "We always like those who admire us; we do not always like those whom we admire."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The gratitude of most men is but a secret desire of receiving greater benefits."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Lovers are never tired of each other, though they always speak of themselves."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "We pardon in the degree that we love."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "We hardly find any persons of good sense save those who agree with us."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The greatest fault of a penetrating wit is to go beyond the mark."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "We may give advice, but we cannot inspire the conduct."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The veracity which increases with old age is not far from folly."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "In their first passion women love their lovers, in all the others they love love."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "Quarrels would not last long if the fault was only on one side."
author: Francis, Duc de la Rochefoucauld

quote: "The opinion of the strongest is always the best."
author: J. de la Fontaine
source: The Wolf and the Lamb. Book i. Fable 10

quote: "By the work one knows the workman."
author: J. de la Fontaine
source: The Hornets and the Bees. Fable 21

quote: "It is a double pleasure to deceive the deceiver."
author: J. de la Fontaine
source: The Cock and the Fox. Book ii. Fable 15

quote: "It is impossible to please all the world and one's father."
author: J. de la Fontaine

quote: "In everything one must consider the end."
author: J. de la Fontaine
source: The Fox and the Gnat. Fable 5

quote: "They are too green," he said, "and only good for fools."
author: J. de la Fontaine
source: The Fox and the Grapes. Fable 11

quote: "Let ignorance talk as it will, learning has its value."
author: J. de la Fontaine
source: The Use of Knowledge. Book viii. Fable 19

quote: "No path of flowers leads to glory."
author: J. de la Fontaine

quote: "The world, dear Agnes, is a strange affair."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière
source: L'École des Femmes. Act ii. Sc. 6

quote: "Although I am a pious man, I am not the less a man."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière

quote: "The real Amphitryon is the Amphitryon who gives dinners."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière

quote: "Tell me to whom you are addressing yourself when you say that. I am addressing myself--I am addressing myself to my cap."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière

quote: "The beautiful eyes of my cash-box."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière

quote: "You are speaking before a man to whom all Naples is known."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière

quote: "I will maintain it before the whole world."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière
source: Le Bourgeois Gentilhomme. Act iv. Sc. 5

quote: "Grammar, which knows how to control even kings."
author: Jean Baptiste Molière
source: Les Femmes savantes. Act ii. Sc. 6

quote: "Ah, there are no longer any children!"
author: Jean Baptiste Molière
source: Le Malade Imaginaire. Act ii. Sc. 11

quote: "Man is but a reed, the weakest in nature, but he is a thinking reed."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "It is not permitted to the most equitable of men to be a judge in his own cause."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "Montaigne is wrong in declaring that custom ought to be followed simply because it is custom, and not because it is reasonable or just."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "Thus we never live, but we hope to live; and always disposing ourselves to be happy, it is inevitable that we never become so."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "If the nose of Cleopatra had been shorter, the whole face of the earth would have been changed."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "The last thing that we find in making a book is to know what we must put first."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "We know the truth, not only by the reason, but also by the heart."
author: Blaise Pascal

quote: "Every age has its pleasures, its style of wit, and its own ways."
author: Nicholas Boileau-despreaux
source: The Art of Poetry. Canto iii. Line 374

quote: "He [Molière] pleases all the world, but cannot please himself."
author: Nicholas Boileau-despreaux

quote: "It may be said that his wit shines at the expense of his memory."
author: Alain René le Sage
source: Gil Blas. Book iii. Chap. xi

quote: "I wish you all sorts of prosperity with a little more taste."
author: Alain René le Sage
source: Gil Blas. Book vii. Chap. iv

quote: "The king [Frederic] has sent me some of his dirty linen to wash; I will wash yours another time."
author: Francis M. Voltaire

quote: "The first who was king was a fortunate soldier: Who serves his country well has no need of ancestors."
author: Francis M. Voltaire

quote: "In the best of possible worlds the château of monseigneur the baron was the most beautiful of châteaux, and madame the best of possible baronesses."
author: Francis M. Voltaire

quote: "In this country [England] it is well to kill from time to time an admiral to encourage the others."
author: Francis M. Voltaire

quote: "The superfluous, a very necessary thing."
author: Francis M. Voltaire

quote: "There are truths which are not for all men, nor for all times."
author: Francis M. Voltaire
source: Letter to Cardinal de Bernis, April 23, 1761

quote: "He [Voltaire] has invented history. It is only the first step which costs."
author: Madame du Deffand
source: In reply to the Cardinal de Polignac

quote: "Days of absence, sad and dreary, Clothed in sorrow's dark array,-- Days of absence, I am weary: She I love is far away."
author: Jean Jacques Rousseau

quote: "See how the world rewards its votaries."
author: Jean Jacques Rousseau

quote: "If the end be well, all is well."
author: Jean Jacques Rousseau

quote: "Whatever you do, do wisely, and think of the consequences."
author: Jean Jacques Rousseau

quote: "Great thoughts come from the heart."
author: Vauvenargues (marquis Of)

quote: "Art is long, life short; judgment difficult, opportunity transient."
author: Goethe
source: Wilhelm Meister. Book vii. Chap. ix

quote: "There is another and a better world."
author: A. F. F. von Kotzebue

quote: "Into the silent land! Ah, who shall lead us thither?"
author: J. G. von Salis

quote: "It is more than a crime; it is a political fault,"--words which I record, because they have been repeated and attributed to others."
author: Joseph Fouché
